quotes.text: Toto, I've a feeling we're not in Kansas anymore.
quotes.speaker: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
quotes.context: Dorothy, having landed in the strange new world of Oz after the tornado, says this to her dog Toto.

quotes.text: You don't apologize to a crowd. You apologize to a person.
quotes.speaker: The Social Network (2010)
quotes.context: Eduardo Saverin, confronting Mark Zuckerberg after a public embarrassment
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the moralistic clarity fits Eduardo's role as Mark's conscience, and Sorkin's staccato rhythm is well-imitated. The giveaway is that it sounds a half-step too epigrammatic; Sorkin usually hides his maxims inside faster overlapping argument.

quotes.text: I've been calm my whole life. Look where it got me.
quotes.speaker: Fargo (1996)
quotes.context: Jerry Lundegaard, unraveling during a phone call as his plan falls apart
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Jerry's pathetic self-pity and his tendency to blame circumstance rather than himself ring true here. The giveaway is that it's a touch too articulate for Jerry; the Coens write him with more stammering deflection and fewer clean sentences.

quotes.text: Every time I look at that water I think about how much of it there is and how little of it matters.
quotes.speaker: Jaws (1975)
quotes.context: Quint, late at night on the Orca before the final hunt
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it imitates Quint's fatalistic, salty monologue register, especially the mood around the Indianapolis speech. The giveaway is that it tips into aphorism territory; Quint's real lines are grittier and more anchored in specific, visceral imagery.

quotes.speaker: L.A. Confidential (1997)
quotes.context: Captain Dudley Smith (James Cromwell) to Jack Vincennes (Kevin Spacey), moments before shooting him.

quotes.text: The world is a business, Mr. Beale. It has been since man crawled out of the slime.
quotes.speaker: Network (1976)
quotes.context: Corporate chairman Arthur Jensen (Ned Beatty) delivering his boardroom sermon to news anchor Howard Beale (Peter Finch).
